data.title: Deflation no more? Japan's largest companies boost pay
data.description:
Some of the most-concrete signs yet that Japan is emerging from decades of near-zero inflation are emerging.

Shunto wage negotiations show most organizations asking wage hikes nearly twice as large as last year. Nikkei yesterday reported on the wage negotiations and offered this table to highlight some of the gains that were achieved.

"Demands submitted by around 3,000 labor unions under the Japanese Trade Union Confederation, or Rengo, averaged 6.09%, an increase of 0.24 percentage points from the previous year, and surpassing the 6% mark forthe first time since 1993, it said last week."

The Bank of Japan next decides on rates on March 19 and the market is 92% priced for no change but a hike to 0.75% is fully priced in by October. Data like this could shift the BOJ into a more-hawkish stance.

data.title: Japan Logistics Fund Asset Manager Takes On Strategic Asset Acquisition, Disposition
data.description:
Japan Logistics Fund said its asset manager Mitsui & Co. Logistics Partners will acquire the Inzai Logistics Center for 1.31 billion yen from SGRealty Co. and dispose of the Komaki Logistics Center for 2.35 billion yen, according to its Wednesday bourse filing.

This decision is part of Japan Logistics Fund's broader growth strategy, aimed at addressing the persistent discount of the investment unit price relative to the NAV per unit.

The Komaki Logistics Center, acquired in 2007 has an internal rate of return (IRR) projected at 5.0% over the holding period, and deemed appropriate for disposition, said the filing.

In September 2024, the Reit set an immediate distribution target of 2,100 yen per unit, which was initially planned for realization over three to four years.

However, to combat ongoing weak unit price trends, Japan Logistics Fund expedited asset acquisition and disposition plans, including a swap and additional asset acquisition in January 2025.

This has resulted in an increase in the distribution per unit forecast for the fiscal period ending July 31, 2025, to 2,150 yen, achieving the target earlier than anticipated.

Shares of the company gained nearly 4% in morning trade Thursday.

data.title: Westpac Bears See Many Reasons to Expect Underperformance — Market Talk
data.description: There are multiple reasons why Westpac is likely to underperform the broader Australian share market in 2025, according to the bank's bears at Morgan Stanley. They highlight eight reasons for their continued underweight rating, but point to three as the most important: margin trends, capital management and the lender's ongoing tech overhaul. The analysts point out in a note to clients that margins and capital both fell short of expectations in Westpac's first fiscal quarter, while investors are waiting on more detail about the tech overhaul benefits. Raising their expense estimates, the MS analysts cut their target price by 6.5% to A$27.30. Shares are down 1.3% at A$29.68. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korean Shares Hit 2-Week High
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.8% to around 2,596 points early Thursday, marking its second consecutive gain to reach a two-week high.

The index tracked Wall Street’s tech-led rally overnight, which was driven by relief over softer-than-expected US inflation data.

Meanwhile, investor sentiment remained fragile as Trump’s steel and aluminum tariff, which took effect on Wednesday, sparked retaliatory measures from Canada and the EU, fueling global trade war fears.

South Korea now also faces a sweeping 25% tariff, as the US scraps its steel and aluminum quota.

In response, the government plans to deliver its stance to the US with Trade Minister Cheong In-kyo taking off for Washington later this week.

Across the index, chipmakers led the charge, with Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix climbing 1.1% and 1.8%, respectively.

data.title: NOVONIX Receives Approval for Purchase of Enterprise South Land for Planned Enterprise South Manufacturing Facility
data.description:
BRISBANE, Australia, March 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — NOVONIX Limited (NASDAQ: NVX, ASX: NVX) (“NOVONIX” or “the Company”), a leading battery materials and technology company, announced today, that it has received unanimous approval from each of the City of Chattanooga and Hamilton County for the purchase of a 182-acre parcel of land for its second mass production plant (“Enterprise South”). Enterprise South will be acquired for approximately US$4.6 million and will be subject to the satisfaction of certain conditions to be specified in the purchase and sale agreement. The Company also received an estimated US$54 million in net tax and other benefits to be realised over a 15-year period and are subject to the satisfaction of a number of conditions. These transactions remain subject to final approval by the Chattanooga Industrial Development Board.

To meet increasing customer demand, the Company previously announced plans for its next large-scale synthetic graphite manufacturing plant in Chattanooga, Tennessee. Enterprise South is expected to reach full production capacity of 31,500 tonnes per annum (“tpa”) by the end of 2028 and is anticipated to create 500 full-time jobs. The Enterprise South facility, together with NOVONIX’s existing Riverside facility which is scaling up to production of 20,000 tpa, is planned to bring the Company’s total production capacity to over 50,000 tpa in Chattanooga by 2028.

Mr. Robert Long, Interim CEO of NOVONIX, said: “We are pleased to announce this next step in our expansion in Chattanooga and deepen our roots in the local community as we continue to grow as a company. We have worked closely with the city, county, and state over the past seven years of operations in Chattanooga. They have been great partners and we, in turn, are proud to strengthen our local hiring and workforce development commitments to the community.”

In addition to the commitments contained in the Company’s local Payment in Lieu of Taxes (PILOT) agreement, NOVONIX also announced the extension and expansion of its local partnership with Lookout Valley Middle High School, NOVONIX Institute of Advanced Battery Technology (NIABT). As part of Hamilton County School’s Future Ready Institutes, the NIABT provides career-focused learning aimed at equipping students with the skills and knowledge they need to succeed in the battery industry and other STEM innovation fields.

In December, the Company announced a conditional commitment from the DOE through the Loan Programs Office (“LPO”) for a direct loan of up to US$754.8 million to be applied towards the financing of NOVONIX Enterprise South.1

NOVONIX’s Riverside facility, also located in Chattanooga, is poised to become the first large-scale production site dedicated to high-performance synthetic graphite for the battery sector in North America. It is slated to begin commercial production in early 2026, with plans to grow output to 20,000 tpa to meet current customer commitments. Previously, the Company announced that the DOE’s Office of Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains (“MESC”) awarded the Company a US$100 million grant2 and that it was selected for a US$103 million investment tax credit3 towards the funding of the Riverside facility. NOVONIX has signed binding offtake agreements to supply synthetic graphite to Panasonic Energy,4 Stellantis,5 and PowerCo,6 committing its full production capacity at its Riverside facility and triggering the need for additional capacity at Enterprise South.

This announcement has been authorised for release by Admiral Robert J Natter, USN Ret., Chairman.

About NOVONIX

NOVONIX is a leading battery technology company revolutionizing the global lithium-ion battery industry with innovative, sustainable technologies, high-performance materials, and more efficient production methods. The Company manufactures industry-leading battery cell testing equipment, is growing its high-performance synthetic graphite material manufacturing operations, and has developed a patented all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis process. Through advanced R&D capabilities, proprietary technology, and strategic partnerships, NOVONIX has gained a prominent position in the electric vehicle and energy storage systems battery industry and is powering a cleaner energy future.

To learn more, visit us at www.novonixgroup.com or on LinkedIn and X.

For NOVONIX Limited

Scott Espenshade, ir@novonixgroup.com (investors)

Stephanie Reid, media@novonixgroup.com (media)

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

This communication contains forward-looking statements about the Company and the industry in which we operate. Forward-looking statements can generally be identified by use of words such as “anticipate,” “believe,” “contemplate,” “continue,” “could,” “estimate,” “expect,” “intend,” “may,” “plan,” “potential,” “predict,” “project,” “should,” “target,” “will,” or “would,” or other similar expressions. Examples of forward-looking statements in this communication include, among others, statements we make regarding our anticipated production capacity and commencement of commercial production at our Riverside facility and the planned Enterprise South facility, our plans to purchase property for and build the new production facility, and our efforts to finance this new production facility with a loan from the LPO.

We have based such statements on our current expectations and projections about future events and trends that we believe may affect our financial condition, results of operations, business strategy and financial needs. Such forward-looking statements involve and are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors which may cause our actual results, performance or achievements to be materially different from any future results, performance or achievements expressed or implied by the forward-looking statements. Such factors include, among others, the timely deployment and scaling of our furnace technology, our ability to meet the technical specifications and demand of our existing and future customers, the accuracy of our estimates regarding market size, expenses, future revenue, capital requirements, needs and access for additional financing, the availability and impact and our compliance with the applicable terms of government support, including the DOE MESC grant and, if a definitive agreement is executed and the loan is funded, the LPO loan, our ability to satisfy the conditions precedent to our entering into definitive loan documents and to the DOE’s funding the LPO loan and, if the loan is obtained, our ability to comply with the restrictions and obligations under the loan documents, our ability to obtain patent rights effective to protect our technologies and processes and successfully defend any challenges to such rights and prevent others from commercializing such technologies and processes, and regulatory developments in the United States, Australia and other jurisdictions. These and other factors that could affect our business and results are included in our filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (“SEC”), including the Company’s most recent annual report on Form 20-F. Copies of these filings may be obtained by visiting our Investor Relations website at www.novonixgroup.com or the SEC’s website at www.sec.gov.

Forward-looking statements are not guarantees of future performance or outcomes, and actual performance and outcomes may differ materially from those made in or suggested by the forward-looking statements contained in this communication. Accordingly, you should not place undue reliance on forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement in this communication is based only on information currently available to us and speaks only as of the date on which it is made. We undertake no obligation to update any forward-looking statement, whether written or oral, that may be made from time to time, whether as a result of new information, future developments or otherwise, except as required by law.

1 NOVONIX Offered Conditional Commitment for US$754 Million Loan from the U.S. Department of Energy for New Synthetic Graphite Manufacturing Plant in Tennessee - NOVONIX

2 NOVONIX Finalizes US$100 Million Grant Award from U.S. Department of Energy - NOVONIX

3 U.S. Government Selects NOVONIX to Receive US$103 Million in Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Tax Credits - NOVONIX

4 Panasonic Energy and NOVONIX Sign Binding Off-Take Agreement - NOVONIX

5 NOVONIX and Stellantis Sign Binding Offtake Agreement - NOVONIX

6 NOVONIX and PowerCo SE Sign Binding Offtake Agreement - NOVONIX

data.title: Noxopharm Taps Australian Facility for Clinical Trial; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Noxopharm engaged Doherty Clinical Trials to conduct the upcoming HERACLES clinical trial in Victoria, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

HERACLES is the first-in-human trial of SOF/SKN, the company's drug candidate for lupus and other autoimmune diseases.

Shares were up 5% in morning trade Thursday.

data.title: Malaysian Shares Rise; Improved Sentiment May Support — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is up 0.4% at 1490.42 in early trade. While broad-based selling pressure has intensified on the bourse recently, sentiment is likely to improve today, supported by U.S. stocks' overnight rebound, analysts at Apex Securities write in a note. Bargain hunting activities could emerge but gains may be tepid given the absence of fresh leads, as global uncertainties continue to dominate, they add. Among the advancers, Gamuda rises 2.4%, YTL Power International gains 2.0% and Sunway is 1.7% higher. Meanwhile, Axiata Group and CelcomDigi each fall 1.7%, and CIMB Group is 0.6% lower. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2113 ET - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is up 0.4% at 1490.42 in early trade. While broad-based selling pressure has intensified on the bourse recently, sentiment is likely to improve today, supported by U.S. stocks' overnight rebound, analysts at Apex Securities write in a note. Bargain hunting activities could emerge but gains may be tepid given the absence of fresh leads, as global uncertainties continue to dominate, they add. Among the advancers, Gamuda rises 2.4%, YTL Power International gains 2.0% and Sunway is 1.7% higher. Meanwhile, Axiata Group and CelcomDigi each fall 1.7%, and CIMB Group is 0.6% lower. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

2106 ET - Continued cost-of-living pressures and economic uncertainty keep Macquarie analysts cautious on Premier Investments following its divestment of several clothing businesses. The analysts write in a note that the discretionary nature and relatively high price point of the conglomerate's remaining Smiggle and Peter Alexander brands mean they are exposed to the impact of inflation and elevated interest rates on consumers. They expect Premier to report first-half Ebit slightly above the midpoint of its A$160 million-A$165 million guidance range. Macquarie cuts its target price 26% to A$21.60 but keeps a neutral rating on the stock, which is down 1.7% at A$20.94. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2103 ET - Rio Tinto's production and free cash flow are likely to accelerate faster than rival BHP's over the rest of this decade, Goldman Sachs analysts say in a note. Alongside valuation, that's why GS prefers Rio's stock over BHP, both of which it rates a buy, the analysts say. Despite both miners spending around US$10 billion-US$11 billion on capex annually, Rio's copper-equivalent production should rise about 20% by the end of the decade. BHP's output is expected to remain largely flat, with its major projects mostly longer dated, say the analysts.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2054 ET - Macquarie analysts turn bearish on Australia's Audinate, citing potential headwinds including trade tariffs. Cutting their recommendation on the stock to underperform from neutral, the analysts tell clients in a note that they see no evidence for optimism on the audio-visual equipment maker. They point to softer economic growth, tariffs, weak market data, and continued discounting of adaptors to support their view. Management is responding soundly to softer demand but these market conditions are set to persist, they add. Macquarie cuts its target price 28% to A$6.30. Shares are down 0.7% at A$6.76. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2042 ET - Orica's A$400 million share buyback confirms the explosives maker's near-term focus is on integrating recent acquisitions, and not pursuing more M&A, Macquarie analysts say in a note. Of Orica's integration efforts, "Terra is performing well following [the] successful Ground Probe acquisition whilst Cyanaco remains [a] work in progress," they say. The analysts were pleased to see the much-anticipated buyback eventuate, even if it was "a case of 'buy the rumor, sell the fact'" for investors, they say. After falling by 1.3% Wednesday, Orica's shares are up 1.3% in Sydney at A$16.895. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2036 ET - The sell-off in U.S. shares this month has some investors starting to sniff returning value. The S&P 500 forward PE ratio fell from 25.3 in December to 20.7 now, says Harry Mamaysky, professor at Columbia Business School and cofounder of QuantStreet Capital. That's been partially driven by the market selloff and partly by increases in earnings estimates, he says. At the same time, the forward PE of European stocks and global stocks ex-U.S. are unchanged from December and trading at a lower PE multiple than the U.S., but the gap is now much lower, Mamaysky says. So on a relative valuation analysis basis, which also considers relative EPS growth rates and dividend yields, the U.S. market looks more compelling. Based on QuantStreet's tactical asset allocation model, the U.S. market is much more attractive than the global counterparts, he adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

2022 ET - South Korea's benchmark Kospi rises 0.6% to 2590.70, as shipbuilding and semiconductor stocks advance. Sentiment is supported by easing U.S. inflation fears after the Labor Department's report on softer-than-expected consumer prices for February. Retail and institutional investors are net buyers. Ship-engine maker HD-Hyundai Marine Engine climbs 8.0% after a new contract win. Hanwha Ocean rises 4.7%, leading a broad rally in shipbuilding stocks. Memory-chip maker SK Hynix adds 1.3%, tracking U.S. semiconductor stocks' rally overnight. USD/KRW is almost flat at 1,451.20 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is up 0.3 basis point at 2.763%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

2014 ET - Japanese stocks are higher as concerns about U.S. inflation ebb for now. Electronics and financial stocks are leading the gains. Advantest is up 5.3% and Rakuten Bank is 4.5% higher. USD/JPY is at 148.35, compared with 148.07 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments in Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 1.1% at 37232.93. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2004 ET - New Zealand investors have dropped the chance of Contact Energy securing regulatory clearance for its takeover of Manawa Energy to less than 40%, Forsyth Barr says. It believes Contact has offered a full price for Manawa, and it will take several years to deliver savings needed to justify the transaction cost. Analyst Andrew Harvey-Green doesn't expect Contact's share price to react much whatever New Zealand's Commerce Commission decides about the deal. "The situation is different for Manawa," Forsyth Barr says. "The passage of time means it is increasingly difficult to estimate the size of the market reaction to no deal." It thinks Manawa's share price could fall to around NZ$4.00 if the takeover is blocked, from NZ$4.88 currently. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1958 ET - New Zealand needs to remain open to diversifying its dairy exports, says Forsyth Barr, citing the slowdown in purchases by its top customer. China accounts for 35% of New Zealand's dairy exports, but local production has been rising at a time when demand is weak. Analyst Matt Montgomerie highlights that China's milk production has increased at a compound annual rate of 5% over the past five years. Its production relative to New Zealand is now at its highest level in two decades. "Rising China milk production and New Zealand's reliance on China (also 40% of Fonterra's EBIT excluding Consumer) remain key risks for the industry," Forsyth Barr says. China remains the weak spot in an otherwise bullish moment for New Zealand dairy producers, with milk prices at record highs and costs stabilizing.(david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1951 ET - Japanese stocks may rise as concerns about U.S. inflation recede for now. Nikkei futures are up 0.9% at 37135 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 148.25, compared with 148.07 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Besides U.S. trade policy, investors are focusing on any developments in Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 36819.09 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1853 ET - Algoma Steel Group says that tariffs on steel and aluminum will have a material and adverse effect on its finances and operations. That is because tariffs have driven steel prices below production costs for much of the industry, as well as heightened general macroeconomic uncertainty. "We remain confident that the Canadian federal and provincial governments will respond swiftly and appropriately to support the industry," CEO Michael Garcia says. "Given the deeply integrated North American supply chain, we believe rational dialogue will prevail between these close allies, restoring normal steel trade." The Canadian company is currently assessing the tariffs, adding that it is too early to estimate the full extent of their effects at this time. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Singapore Shares Edge 0.1% Lower as Investors Digest Mixed Developments — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.1% lower to 3828.84 as investors digest mixed developments. Main theme appears to be relief after U.S. CPI data released Wednesday were slightly below consensus expectations, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. However, there have also been fears of an escalation in the trade war, the analysts add, noting that the EU and Canada announced retaliatory tariffs against the U.S. overnight. Among the worst performers on the benchmark index, Yangzijiang Shipbuilding falls 2.6%, DFI Retail Group loses 1.75%, and Mapletree Industrial Trust is down 1.5%. Meanwhile, City Developments rises 3.6% and Hongkong Land adds 1.8%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
Stocks Fluctuate on Muted Inflation Data, Loud Trade Fight

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended higher after investors weighed the latest trade skirmish with Canada and a solid inflation report. The Dow industrials edged down.

U.S. Federal Deficit Exceeds $1 Trillion Over 5 Months

Data disclosed by the Treasury Department showed an adjusted gap between federal spending and revenue of $1.063 trillion for October through February.

Trump set to nominate Michelle Bowman as Fed's top bank supervisor

President Donald Trump is set to nominate Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman to be the central bank's top bank supervisor, a White House official confirmed to MarketWatch.

Europe, Canada Hit Back at U.S. Steel Tariffs

The EU is targeting a range of American goods, including whiskey and motorcycles, with 50% tariffs, taking aim at politically sensitive products.

Inflation Cooled to 2.8% in February, Lower Than Expected

Inflation eased last month, but the data may offer less comfort to businesses, consumers and the Fed than it otherwise would as looming tariffs may keep prices rising.

Bank of Canada Cuts Policy Rate to 2.75% as Economy Faces 'New Crisis'

The central bank must balance the risk of tariff-fueled higher inflation and the damage to growth from U.S. trade policy, Gov. Tiff Macklem says.

WTO's Trade Barometer Picks Up, Pointing to Resilient Flows

The World Trade Organization's trade barometer rose, but it warned increased purchases by those seeking to get ahead of threatened tariffs might exaggerate the health of the global trading system.

Wind and Solar Overtake Coal Power for First Time in U.S.

The two renewable energy sources accounted for 17% of the country's power mix while coal fell to a low of 15%. Natural gas remains by far the largest source of electricity, accounting for 43%.

Senate GOP's Tax-Cut Wishlist Heads North of $5 Trillion

On the menu: more than 200 ideas, ranging from the estate tax to housing credits and loosening the cap on the state and local tax deduction.

OPEC Sticks to Oil-Demand View Ahead of Output Hike

The cartel still expects demand to grow by 1.45 million barrels a day this year and 1.43 million barrels a day the next, bolstered by strong air travel demand and road mobility.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
Intel Names Lip-Bu Tan as Chief Executive Officer

The former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, steps in as the semiconductor manufacturer has lost market share.

Adobe Stock Slides Despite Earnings Beat. The CFO Is Confident About AI.

Adobe posted first-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.08 a share, beating estimates of $4.97 a share.

LVMH Taps Arnault's Son to Head Loro Piana in Leadership Reshuffle

The fashion role is expected to bolster Frédéric Arnault's credentials as a candidate to one day lead the luxury-goods giant.

Puma Will Cut 500 Positions Globally as Part of a Cost-Cutting Plan

Job reductions will be implemented at the group's headquarters in Germany and in regional offices. The company has a total workforce of 22,500.

D-Wave Claims 'Quantum Supremacy,' Beating Traditional Computers

D-Wave is the latest quantum computing company to say it has achieved "quantum supremacy," better known as the ability to solve a problem with a quantum computer that can't be done with a traditional computer.

Niantic to Sell Pokemon GO, Other Games to Saudi-Backed Group in $3.5 Billion Deal

Niantic is also selling "Pikmin Bloom," and "Monster Hunter Now," Niantic's most recent game.

Sunnova Prepares Restructuring Talks That Could Include Bankruptcy Filing

Sunnova Energy, one of the country's largest residential solar companies, is preparing to engage with creditors to explore options including a potential bankruptcy filing over its roughly $8.5 billion in debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

Wonder Acquires Tastemade for Its Latest Quest: to Make a 'Mealtime Super App'

Wonder CEO Marc Lore hopes the company will one day offer AI-powered autonomous food ordering for all three meals of the day.

Whiskey Is a Target of Europe's Retaliatory Tariffs. Brown-Forman Stock Slides.

The European Union plans to establish countermeasures in April that apply to products ranging from boats to bourbon.

Target Beefs Up Grocery Supply Chain as Consumer Spending Shifts

The retailer is bringing its supply chain for fresh and frozen foods in house to better forecast demand and plan inventory.

data.title: Aussie copper miners surge on boost in commodity prices
data.description:
** Australian copper miners surge on the back of copper prices touching multi-month highs

** Copper prices rise as traders prepared for potential U.S. tariffs, while investor sentiment got a boost from signs of improving demand in China and a possible ceasefire in Ukraine

** Copper major Sandfire Resources gains as much as 3.6% to A$10.85, its largest intraday pct gain since March 6

** Canada-based miner Capstone Copper Corp's Australia shares rises as much as 3.9% to A$8.85, its highest level since March 7

data.title: Mitsui Fudosan Logistics Park Secures 1 Billion Yen Loans
data.description:
Mitsui Fudosan Logistics Park has secured a total of 1 billion yen in new borrowings, according to its Wednesday bourse filing.

The Bank of Fukuoka will provide 400 million yen with a base rate plus 0.14% interest, repayable by March 15, 2032.

Mitsui Sumitomo Insurance will lend 600 million yen with a base rate plus 0.09% interest, repayable by Sept. 14, 2029.

The funds, which will be obtained March 14, will be used for repayment purposes.

data.title: Premier Investments Exposed to Cost-of-Living Pressures — Market Talk
data.description: Continued cost-of-living pressures and economic uncertainty keep Macquarie analysts cautious on Premier Investments following its divestment of several clothing businesses. The analysts write in a note that the discretionary nature and relatively high price point of the conglomerate's remaining Smiggle and Peter Alexander brands mean they are exposed to the impact of inflation and elevated interest rates on consumers. They expect Premier to report first-half Ebit slightly above the midpoint of its A$160 million-A$165 million guidance range. Macquarie cuts its target price 26% to A$21.60 but keeps a neutral rating on the stock, which is down 1.7% at A$20.94. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold firms as tariff uncertainty, cooler inflation data lend support
data.description:
Gold prices edged higher on Thursday as uncertainty over tariffs persisted, driving safe-haven demand, while a cooler-than-expected U.S. inflation print also supported bullion by strengthening expectations of rate cuts.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold was up 0.2% at $2,938.24 an ounce, as of 0050 GMT, while U.S. gold futures were steady at $2,945.70.

* Data showed that the U.S. consumer price index increased less than expected last month, but the improvement is likely temporary against the backdrop of aggressive tariffs on imports that are expected to raise the cost of most goods in the months ahead.

* Lower inflation leaves more room for the U.S. Federal Reserve to cut interest rates, and non-yielding gold thrives in a low-interest rate setting.

* Trump early this month triggered a trade war, increasing the tariffs on goods from China to 20% and imposing a new 25% duty on Canadian and Mexican imports, before dialling back and providing a one month exemption for some goods that meet the rules of origin under the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement on trade.

* Trump also reversed course on Tuesday afternoon on a pledge to double tariffs on steel and aluminum from Canada to 50%, hours after announcing the higher tariffs.

* Trump's tariffs are widely expected to stoke inflation and economic uncertainty, and have prompted gold to reach a record high of $2,956.15 on February 24.

* Gold is seen as a hedge against political risks and inflation.

* Investors now await U.S. Producer Price Index (CPI) data due later in the day to gain further insights into the Fed's monetary policy.

* Spot silver rose 0.2% to $33.29 an ounce, platinum added 0.2% to $985.18, and palladium gained 0.6% to $954.63.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

1230 US Initial Jobless Clm 8 March, w/e

1230 US PPI Machine Manuf'ing Feb

1430 US EIA-Nat Gas Chg Bcf, Nat Gas-EIA Implied Flow 7 Mar, w/e
